item.title: US private payrolls increase steadily in March, ADP says
item.description: U.S. private payrolls increased steadily in March, the ADP's national employment report ​showed on Wednesday.

item.title: India's Wipro names Nagendra Bandaru as CEO of AI segment
item.description: Indian ⁠IT ​services firm ​Wipro on ​Wednesday ​named insider Nagendra ‌Bandaru ⁠as the ​CEO ​of ⁠its ​AI ​segment.

item.title: Exclusive: Fed's Barkin says households, firms still see oil shock through a "short-term lens"
item.description: There is little evidence yet that consumers have pulled back on spending, Barkin said.

item.title: SpaceX seen as make-or-break test for mega IPOs
item.description: The global IPO market has needed a win for years and Elon Musk's SpaceX could be the breakthrough. The last company to make a market debut at over a trillion dollar valuation was Saudi Aramco in 2019.

item.title: Swiss finance minister sues for defamation over Grok-created post
item.description: Swiss Finance Minister Karin Keller-Sutter has filed a criminal complaint for defamation and insult after an X user published an obscene post about her ​created by Elon Musk's chatbot Grok, a spokesperson for her ministry ‌said on Wednesday.

item.title: Coca-Cola plans to invest $1 billion in South Africa through 2030
item.description: Coca-Cola and its two authorized bottlers, ​Coca-Cola Beverages South Africa ‌and Coca-Cola Peninsula Beverages, will invest 17.6 billion rand ($1.05 billion) ​in South Africa ​through 2030, to support expanded ⁠production capacity, the company ​said.

item.title: Exclusive: Worries over science, new addicts stymie US nicotine pouch fast-track scheme
item.description: Popular nicotine pouch products have yet to be cleared for sale in the ​United States despite a fast-track Food and Drug Administration scheme, as agency scientists hesitate to authorise them due to potential risks to new users, including ‌children, three sources told Reuters.

item.title: War-driven US dollar rebound to fade as broad safe-haven appeal erodes
item.description: The dollar's modest rebound since the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran started a little over a month ago will fade as its safe-haven appeal erodes, according to currency strategists in a Reuters poll who stuck to their long-held view the ​currency will weaken.

item.title: Boeing, Lockheed Martin to boost production of PAC-3 MSE seekers, Pentagon says
item.description: The ​Pentagon on ‌Wednesday said ​it ​had reached a ⁠framework ​seven-year ​deal with Boeing Co ​and ​Lockheed Martin Corp ‌to ⁠triple production capacity ​for ​Patriot ⁠Advanced Capability-3 ​Missile ​Segment ⁠Enhancement seekers.

item.title: Former German finance minister Joerg Kukies to join Morgan Stanley
item.description: Joerg Kukies, a former German finance minister, ​will take a role at Morgan Stanley, the ‌U.S. bank said on Wednesday, marking a return to the senior ranks of ​banking after years of government ​service.

item.title: US mortgage rates jump to 6.57%, highest since August, MBA says
item.description: The interest rate on the most popular U.S. home loan jumped last week to the highest since August, as rising oil prices from ​the U.S. and Israeli war on Iran fueled inflation fears ‌and drove up yields on the Treasury bonds that lenders widely use as benchmarks for setting mortgage rates.

item.title: Mega IPOs set to test US market depth despite volatility
item.description: Companies raised the most through share sales in the ​first quarter since 2021, and mega-IPOs from Space X and OpenAI may raise tens of billions more in the coming months despite ‌volatility amid the Iran war and a software stock selloff, according to new data and dealmakers.

item.title: Morning Bid: Finding the 'off ramp'
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: US first-quarter auto sales expected to slip on affordability concerns
item.description: New vehicle sales in the U.S. are expected to fall in the first quarter of 2026, as economic uncertainty, high borrowing costs and vehicle prices keep buyers on the sidelines.

item.title: Nike's turnaround put to test as Middle East conflict poses new risks
item.description: Nike's efforts to steady its business face a fresh setback, with executives cautioning that unrest in the Middle East could further complicate the turnaround, while the sportswear ​giant still struggles to regain traction in China.

item.title: A New York vintner raids US wine cellars to skirt Trump's tariffs
item.description: New York wine retailer Chris Leon has a novel strategy to avoid the steep tariffs that have ‌been slapped on imported wines from France and Italy under U.S. President Donald Trump: raiding American wine cellars for bottles already in the country.

item.title: Exclusive: Shell in advanced talks with Venezuela for more gas areas
item.description: Shell is in advanced talks with Venezuela's government to develop four large areas near Trinidad and Tobago, in two of the South ​American country's largest offshore natural gas fields, two people familiar with the discussions said.

item.title: Tesla first-quarter deliveries likely to dip sequentially as EV demand softens
item.description: Tesla's first-quarter deliveries are expected to be lower than in the December quarter as ​the electric-vehicle maker struggles with uneven demand ‌and intensifying competition in key markets.

item.title: US stock futures climb as Iran war de-escalation optimism lifts sentiment
item.description: U.S. stock futures advanced on Wednesday, after the indexes ​saw their biggest one-day gains in nearly a year in the previous session, following President Donald ‌Trump's comments that suggested a swift end to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Global first-quarter M&A exceeds $1.2 trillion, led by AI
item.description: Turbulence linked to the war in Iran and swings in valuations have yet to deter corporate deal-making as transactions in the first quarter exceeded a record of $1.2 trillion, LSEG data shows, and dealmakers ​say much more is in the pipeline.

item.title: China venture capital funding set to hit record in Q1 on state-led tech push
item.description: Fundraising in China's venture capital (VC) industry is set to rise to ​a record in the first quarter, data showed, as Beijing doubles down on investments in the technology sector, ‌including in AI and robotics.

item.title: Chinese chipmakers claim nearly half of local market as Nvidia's lead shrinks
item.description: Chinese GPU and AI chip ‌makers captured nearly 41% of China's AI accelerator server market last year, eroding Nvidia's once-dominant position in one of its most important overseas markets, according to data from an IDC ​report reviewed by Reuters.

item.title: PE firm Arcline walks away from bid for UK's Senior as other suitors circle
item.description: The field of suitors for British aerospace and ​defence supplier Senior Plc narrowed after private ‌equity firm Arcline Investment Management dropped out of buyout talks on Wednesday, though negotiations with other bidders remained ongoing.

item.title: New BP CEO takes helm pledging consistency, staff note shows
item.description: BP's new CEO Meg O'Neill told employees on Wednesday she would offer consistency while accelerating the group's performance, a year after BP pivoted its strategy firmly back to oil and ​gas, according to a staff note seen by Reuters.

item.title: Euro zone factory growth hits 45-month high amid supply disruptions, PMI shows
item.description: Euro zone manufacturing growth bounced to its strongest in nearly four years in March as supply chain disruptions inflated growth figures although underlying demand remained ​tepid and soaring input costs due to the Iran warthreatened to undermine the sector's ‌fragile recovery, a survey showed.

item.title: Microsoft on track to invest $5.5 billion in Singapore by 2029, WSJ reports
item.description: Microsoft is ​on track ‌to invest $5.5 billion ​in ​cloud and artificial ⁠intelligence ​infrastructure in ​Singapore through 2029, the ​Wall ​Street Journal reported ‌on ⁠Wednesday.

item.title: Tesla French car registrations triple in March
item.description: Tesla new car registrations in France more than tripled in March and were just shy of an all-time high recorded ​over two years ago, national data showed on ‌Wednesday in the latest sign of the EV giant's sales recovery in Europe.

item.title: China's CAS Space targets $607 million IPO for reusable rocket R&D
item.description: China's CAS Space Technology is seeking to raise around 4.18 billion yuan ($607 million) in a planned listing on ​Shanghai's tech-focussed STAR Market, according to a filing on Tuesday, ‌as Chinese space firms take advantage of government support to pursue initial public offerings.

item.title: Baidu robotaxi outage in Wuhan caused by 'system failure', police say
item.description: A "system failure" caused a robotaxi outage involving multiple vehicles operated by Baidu's Apollo Go in central China's Wuhan, local police ​said on Wednesday, re-igniting safety concerns over the fast-growing service.

item.title: Morning Bid: April fools rush in
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Gregor Stuart Hunter:
